toolSpec.name: search_faq
toolSpec.description: Search authoritative Weekly Thing FAQ entries. Use first for questions about subscribing, unsubscribing, cost, membership, RSS, schedule, breaks, archive access, search, Thingy, privacy, community, sharing, contact, site tooling, and how the newsletter works.

toolSpec.name: search_archive
toolSpec.description: Hybrid search over Weekly Thing chunks. Default first stop for broad topics, themes, and evidence gathering. Use iteratively — refine the query and year_range based on what comes back. year_range accepts a [start, end] array, a single "YYYY" string, or {"start": YYYY, "end": YYYY}.

toolSpec.name: get_issue
toolSpec.description: Return one full Weekly Thing issue with section structure preserved. Use when you need full context for a specific issue number you already have.

toolSpec.name: get_section
toolSpec.description: Return a named section from one issue (e.g., "Notable", "Briefly", "Featured"). Cheaper than get_issue when you only need one section.

toolSpec.name: find_links
toolSpec.description: Query the editorial link graph by domain, topic/entity, and year range. Does not fetch linked pages. Call without filters to retrieve top-cited domains across the archive. year_range accepts a [start, end] array, a "YYYY" string, or {"start": YYYY, "end": YYYY}.

toolSpec.name: domain_history
toolSpec.description: List every issue that cited a domain with date and surrounding link context. Use for "how often / when / in what context did Jamie link to X" questions.

toolSpec.name: quote_search
toolSpec.description: Exact substring search across issue bodies. Use to verify whether a specific phrase, name, or product actually appears in the archive — do not infer coverage from related search_archive hits.

toolSpec.name: list_issues
toolSpec.description: Metadata-only issue listing filtered by year, topic, or entity. Use for counts and aggregate pattern questions where body chunks are not needed (e.g., "how many issues mention X").

toolSpec.name: compare_eras
toolSpec.description: Search the same topic in two year windows and return both result sets. Use for two-window before/after contrasts. For three or more windows (early/middle/recent), call search_archive separately with year_range for each. year_a and year_b accept a [start, end] array, a "YYYY" string, or {"start": YYYY, "end": YYYY}.
